25	#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, Diagnostic)]
26	#[error("Multiple channel priorities are not allowed in a single environment")]
27	pub struct ChannelPriorityCombinationError;
28	
29	/// A trait that implement various methods for collections that combine
30	/// attributes of Features It is implemented by Environment, GroupedEnvironment
31	/// and SolveGroup. Remove some of the boilerplate of combining features and its
32	/// derived data from multiple sources.
33	///
34	/// The name of the lifetime parameter is named `'source` that the borrow comes
35	/// from the source of the data for most implementations this will be the pixi
36	/// project.
37	///
38	/// There is blanket implementation available for all types that implement
39	/// [`HasWorkspaceManifest`] and [`HasFeaturesIter`]
40	pub trait FeaturesExt<'source>: HasWorkspaceManifest<'source> + HasFeaturesIter<'source> {
41	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection, preserving the
42	    /// effective metadata for the first winning entry of every unique channel.
43	    fn prioritized_channels(
44	        &self,
45	    ) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
46	        let channels = self.features().flat_map(|feature| match &feature.channels {
47	            Some(channels) => channels,
48	            None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.channels,
49	        });
50	
51	        let mut prioritized = IndexMap::new();
52	        for channel in channels.into_iter().sorted_by(|a, b| {
53	            let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
54	            let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
55	            b.cmp(&a)
56	        }) {
57	            prioritized.entry(&channel.channel).or_insert(channel);
58	        }
59	
60	        prioritized
61	    }
62	
63	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
64	    ///
65	    /// Users can specify custom channels on a per-feature basis. This method
66	    /// collects and deduplicates all the channels from all the features in
67	    /// the order they are defined in the manifest.
68	    ///
69	    /// If a feature does not specify any channel the default channels from the
70	    /// project metadata are used instead.
71	    fn channels(&self) -> IndexSet<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl> {
72	        self.prioritized_channels().into_keys().collect()
73	    }
74	
75	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
76	    ///
77	    /// This function is similar to [`Self::channels]` but it resolves the
78	    /// channel urls using the provided channel config.
79	    fn channel_urls(
80	        &self,
81	        channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
82	    ) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
83	        self.channels()
84	            .into_iter()
85	            .cloned()
86	            .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
87	            .collect()
88	    }
89	
90	    /// Returns the channel priority, error on multiple, different values, return None if
91	    /// no value is set.
92	    ///
93	    /// When using multiple channel priorities over different features we should
94	    /// error as the user should decide what they want.
95	    fn channel_priority(&self) -> Result<Option<ChannelPriority>, ChannelPriorityCombinationError> {
96	        let mut channel_priority = None;
97	        for feature in self.features() {
98	            if let Some(priority) = feature.channel_priority {
99	                // If we already have a priority and it's different, error
100	                if channel_priority.is_some() && channel_priority != Some(priority) {
101	                    return Err(ChannelPriorityCombinationError);
102	                }
103	                channel_priority = Some(priority);
104	            }
105	        }
106	        Ok(channel_priority)
107	    }
108	
109	    /// Returns the raw workspace exclude-newer configuration before channel and
110	    /// package-specific overrides are applied.
111	    fn exclude_newer_raw(&self) -> Option<ExcludeNewer> {
112	        self.workspace_manifest().workspace.exclude_newer
113	    }
114	
115	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration.
116	    fn exclude_newer_config(
117	        &self,
118	    ) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
119	        self.exclude_newer_config_resolved()
120	            .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
121	    }
122	
123	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
124	    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
125	        &self,
126	    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
127	        let mut exclude_newer = self
128	            .exclude_newer_raw()
129	            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));
130	
131	        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
132	            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
133	                continue;
134	            };
135	
136	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
137	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
138	            });
139	
140	            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
141	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
142	                    .clone()
143	                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
144	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
145	                    channel.channel.to_string(),
146	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
147	                ),
148	            };
149	        }
150	
151	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
152	            .workspace_manifest()
153	            .workspace
154	            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
155	        {
156	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
157	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
158	            });
159	
160	            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
161	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
162	                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
163	                }
164	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
165	                    .clone()
166	                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
167	            };
168	        }
169	
170	        Ok(exclude_newer)
171	    }
172	
173	    /// Returns the effective PyPI exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
174	    fn pypi_exclude_newer_config_resolved(&self) -> ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer {
175	        let mut exclude_newer = self
176	            .exclude_newer_raw()
177	            .map(|config| ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()))
178	            .unwrap_or_default();
179	
180	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
181	            .workspace_manifest()
182	            .workspace
183	            .pypi_exclude_newer_package_overrides
184	        {
185	            exclude_newer = match package_exclude_newer {
186	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
187	                    exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(name.as_normalized().clone(), *dt)
188	                }
189	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(
190	                    name.as_normalized().clone(),
191	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff(),
192	                ),
193	            };
194	        }
195	
196	        exclude_newer
197	    }
198	
199	    /// Returns the strategy for solving packages.
200	    ///
201	    /// The chosen strategy is the first explicitly declared one in a feature
202	    /// as they are provided by the [`HasFeaturesIter::features`] iterator.
203	    ///
204	    /// If no feature declares a strategy, the default value of [`SolveStrategy`] is used.
205	    fn solve_strategy(&self) -> SolveStrategy {
206	        self.features()
207	            .flat_map(|feature| feature.solve_strategy)
208	            .next()
209	            .unwrap_or_default()
210	    }
211	
212	    /// Returns the platforms that this collection is compatible with.
213	    ///
214	    /// Which platforms a collection support depends on which platforms the
215	    /// selected features of the collection supports. The platforms that are
216	    /// supported by the collection is the intersection of the platforms
217	    /// supported by its features.
218	    ///
219	    /// Features can specify which platforms they support through the
220	    /// `platforms` key. If a feature does not specify any platforms the
221	    /// features defined by the project are used.
222	    fn platforms(&self) -> HashSet<Platform> {
223	        self.features()
224	            .map(|feature| {
225	                match &feature.platforms {
226	                    Some(platforms) => platforms,
227	                    None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.platforms,
228	                }
229	                .iter()
230	                .copied()
231	                .collect::<HashSet<_>>()
232	            })
233	            .reduce(|accumulated_platforms, feat| {
234	                accumulated_platforms.intersection(&feat).copied().collect()
235	            })
236	            .unwrap_or_default()
237	    }
238	
239	    /// Returns the system requirements for this collection.
240	    ///
241	    /// The system requirements of the collection are the union of the system
242	    /// requirements of all the features in the collection. If multiple
243	    /// features specify a requirement for the same system package, the
244	    /// highest is chosen.
245	    fn local_system_requirements(&self) -> SystemRequirements {
246	        self.features()
247	            .map(|feature| &feature.system_requirements)
248	            .fold(SystemRequirements::default(), |acc, req| {
249	                acc.union(req)
250	                    .expect("system requirements should have been validated upfront")
251	            })
252	    }
253	
254	    /// Returns true if any of the features has any reference to a pypi
255	    /// dependency.
256	    fn has_pypi_dependencies(&self) -> bool {
257	        self.features().any(|f| f.has_pypi_dependencies())
258	    }
259	
260	    /// Returns the PyPi dependencies to install for this collection.
261	    ///
262	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
263	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
264	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
265	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
266	    fn pypi_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> PyPiDependencies {
267	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
268	            .features()
269	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
270	            .filter_map(|f| f.pypi_dependencies(platform))
271	            .collect();
272	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
273	    }
274	
275	    /// Returns the dependencies to install for this collection.
276	    ///
277	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
278	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
279	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
280	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
281	    ///
282	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
283	    /// into consideration.
284	    fn dependencies(&self, kind: SpecType, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
285	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
286	            .features()
287	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
288	            .filter_map(|f| f.dependencies(kind, platform))
289	            .collect();
290	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
291	    }
292	
293	    /// Returns the combined dependencies to install for this collection.
294	    ///
295	    /// The `build` dependencies overwrite the `host` dependencies which
296	    /// overwrite the `run` dependencies.
297	    ///
298	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
299	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
300	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
301	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
302	    ///
303	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
304	    /// into consideration.
305	    fn combined_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
306	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
307	            .features()
308	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
309	            .filter_map(|f| f.combined_dependencies(platform))
310	            .collect();
311	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
312	    }
313	
314	    /// Returns the combined dev dependencies to install for this collection.
315	    ///
316	    /// Dev dependencies from all features in the group are collected and
317	    /// merged. If multiple features define the same dev dependency, the
318	    /// last one wins (later features override earlier ones).
319	    fn combined_dev_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDevDependencies {
320	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
321	            .features()
322	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
323	            .filter_map(|f| f.dev_dependencies(platform))
324	            .collect();
325	
326	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
327	    }
328	
329	    /// Returns the combined version constraints for this collection.
330	    ///
331	    /// Constraints from all features are combined. If multiple features define
332	    /// a constraint for the same package, all constraints are retained and the
333	    /// solver must satisfy all of them simultaneously.
334	    ///
335	    /// If the `platform` is `None`, no platform specific constraints are taken
336	    /// into consideration.
337	    fn combined_constraints(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaConstraints {
338	        let constraints: Vec<_> = self
339	            .features()
340	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
341	            .filter_map(|f| f.constraints(platform))
342	            .collect();
343	        DependencyMap::merge_all(constraints.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
344	    }
345	
346	    /// Returns the pypi options for this collection.
347	    ///
348	    /// The pypi options of all features are combined. They will be combined in
349	    /// the order that they are defined in the manifest.
350	    /// The index-url is a special case and can only be defined once. This
351	    /// should have been verified beforehand.
352	    fn pypi_options(&self) -> PypiOptions {
353	        // Collect all the pypi-options from the features in one set,
354	        // deduplicate them and sort them on feature index, default feature comes last.
355	        let pypi_options: Vec<_> = self
356	            .features()
357	            .filter_map(|feature| {
358	                if feature.pypi_options().is_none() {
359	                    self.workspace_manifest().workspace.pypi_options.as_ref()
360	                } else {
361	                    feature.pypi_options()
362	                }
363	            })
364	            .collect();
365	
366	        // Merge all the pypi options into one.
367	        pypi_options
368	            .into_iter()
369	            .fold(PypiOptions::default(), |acc, opts| {
370	                acc.union(opts)
371	                    .expect("merging of pypi-options should already have been checked")
372	            })
373	    }

102	            platform: Platform::current(),

104	            virtual_packages: vec![],
105	            strategy: SolveStrategy::default(),
106	            channel_priority: ChannelPriority::default(),

113	impl SolveCondaEnvironmentSpec {
114	    /// Solves this environment
115	    pub async fn solve(
116	        self,
117	    ) -> Result<Vec<PixiRecord>, CommandDispatcherError<SolveCondaEnvironmentError>> {
118	        // Solving is a CPU-intensive task, we spawn this on a background task to allow
119	        // for more concurrency.
120	        let solve_result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
121	            let exclude_newer = self.exclude_newer.clone().map(Into::into);
122	
123	            // Determine for which records we have source records because those records should only
124	            //  be installed as source records.
125	            let package_names_from_source = self
126	                .source_repodata
127	                .iter()
128	                .flat_map(|metadata| &metadata.records)
129	                .map(|metadata| &metadata.package_record.name)
130	                .dedup()
131	                .collect::<HashSet<_>>();
132	
133	            // Filter all installed packages
134	            let installed = self
135	                .installed
136	                .into_iter()
137	                // Only lock binary records
138	                .filter_map(|record| record.into_binary())
139	                // Filter any record we want as a source record
140	                .filter(|record| !package_names_from_source.contains(&record.package_record.name))
141	                .collect();
142	
143	            // Create direct dependencies on the source packages to feed to the solver.
144	            let source_match_specs = self
145	                .source_specs
146	                .into_specs()
147	                .map(|(name, spec)| {
148	                    MatchSpec::from_nameless(spec.to_nameless_match_spec(), name.into())
149	                })
150	                .collect::<Vec<_>>();
151	
152	            let binary_match_specs = self
153	                .binary_specs
154	                .into_match_specs(&self.channel_config)
155	                .map_err(SolveCondaEnvironmentError::SpecConversionError)?;
156	
157	            let constrains_match_specs = self
158	                .constraints
159	                .into_match_specs(&self.channel_config)
160	                .map_err(SolveCondaEnvironmentError::SpecConversionError)?;
161	
162	            // Create match specs for dev source packages themselves
163	            // Use a special prefix to avoid name clashes with real packages
164	            // When multiple variants exist for the same package, we only create one match spec
165	            // and let the solver choose which variant to use based on the constraints.
166	            // TODO: It would be nicer if the rattler solver could handle this directly
167	            // by introducing a special type of name/package for these virtual dependencies
168	            // that represent "install my dependencies but not me" packages.
169	            let dev_source_match_specs: Vec<_> = self
170	                .dev_source_records
171	                .iter()
172	                .map(|dev_source| dev_source.name.clone())
173	                .collect::<std::collections::HashSet<_>>()
174	                .into_iter()
175	                .map(|name| {
176	                    let prefixed_name = format!("__pixi_dev_source_{}", name.as_normalized());
177	                    MatchSpec {
178	                        name: rattler_conda_types::PackageName::new_unchecked(prefixed_name).into(),
179	                        ..MatchSpec::default()
180	                    }
181	                })
182	                .collect();
183	
184	            // Construct repodata records for source records and dev sources so that we can feed them to the
185	            // solver.
186	            let mut url_to_source_package = HashMap::new();
187	            let mut url_to_dev_source = HashMap::new();
188	
189	            // Add source records
190	            for source_metadata in &self.source_repodata {
191	                for record in &source_metadata.records {
192	                    let url = unique_url(record);
193	                    let repodata_record = RepoDataRecord {
194	                        package_record: record.package_record.clone(),
195	                        url: url.clone(),
196	                        identifier: DistArchiveIdentifier {
197	                            identifier: ArchiveIdentifier {
198	                                name: record.package_record.name.as_normalized().to_string(),
199	                                version: record.package_record.version.to_string(),
200	                                build_string: format!("{}_source", record.package_record.build),
201	                            },
202	                            archive_type: CondaArchiveType::Conda.into(),
203	                        },
204	                        channel: None,
205	                    };
206	                    let mut record = record.clone();
207	                    record.build_source = source_metadata.source.build_source().cloned();
208	                    url_to_source_package.insert(url, (record, repodata_record));
209	                }
210	            }
211	
212	            // Collect all dev source names for filtering
213	            let dev_source_names: std::collections::HashSet<_> = self
214	                .dev_source_records
215	                .iter()
216	                .map(|ds| ds.name.clone())
217	                .collect();
218	
219	            // Add dev source records
220	            for dev_source in &self.dev_source_records {
221	                let url = unique_dev_source_url(dev_source);
222	                let prefixed_name =
223	                    format!("__pixi_dev_source_{}", dev_source.name.as_normalized());
224	                let build_string = dev_source_build_string(dev_source);
225	                let repodata_record = RepoDataRecord {
226	                    package_record: rattler_conda_types::PackageRecord {
227	                        subdir: self.platform.to_string(),
228	                        depends: dev_source
229	                            .dependencies
230	                            .iter_specs()
231	                            .filter(|(name, _)| !dev_source_names.contains(*name))
232	                            .map(|(name, spec)| {
233	                                let nameless = spec
234	                                    .clone()
235	                                    .try_into_nameless_match_spec_ref(&self.channel_config)
236	                                    .unwrap_or_default();
237	                                MatchSpec::from_nameless(nameless, name.clone().into()).to_string()
238	                            })
239	                            .collect(),
240	                        constrains: dev_source
241	                            .constraints
242	                            .iter_specs()
243	                            .filter(|(name, _)| !dev_source_names.contains(*name))
244	                            .filter_map(|(name, spec)| {
245	                                let nameless = spec
246	                                    .clone()
247	                                    .try_into_nameless_match_spec(&self.channel_config)
248	                                    .ok()?;
249	                                Some(
250	                                    MatchSpec::from_nameless(nameless, name.clone().into())
251	                                        .to_string(),
252	                                )
253	                            })
254	                            .collect(),
255	                        ..rattler_conda_types::PackageRecord::new(
256	                            rattler_conda_types::PackageName::new_unchecked(prefixed_name.clone()),
257	                            Version::major(0),
258	                            build_string.clone(),
259	                        )
260	                    },
261	                    url: url.clone(),
262	                    identifier: DistArchiveIdentifier {
263	                        identifier: ArchiveIdentifier {
264	                            name: prefixed_name.clone(),
265	                            version: "0".to_string(),
266	                            build_string: format!("{build_string}_devsource"),
267	                        },
268	                        archive_type: CondaArchiveType::Conda.into(),
269	                    },
270	                    channel: None,
271	                };
272	                url_to_dev_source.insert(url, (dev_source, repodata_record));
273	            }
274	
275	            // Collect repodata records from the remote servers, source metadata, and dev sources
276	            // together. The source and dev source records go into the first "channel" to ensure
277	            // they are picked first.
278	            //
279	            // TODO: This only holds up when the channel priority is strict. We should
280	            // probably enforce this better somehow..
281	            let mut solvable_records = Vec::with_capacity(self.binary_repodata.len() + 1);
282	            solvable_records.push(
283	                url_to_source_package
284	                    .values()
285	                    .map(|(_, record)| record)
286	                    .chain(url_to_dev_source.values().map(|(_, record)| record))
287	                    .collect_vec(),
288	            );
289	            for repo_data in &self.binary_repodata {
290	                solvable_records.push(repo_data.iter().collect_vec());
291	            }
292	
293	            // Construct a solver task that we can start solving.
294	            let task = rattler_solve::SolverTask {
295	                specs: source_match_specs
296	                    .into_iter()
297	                    .chain(binary_match_specs)
298	                    .chain(dev_source_match_specs)
299	                    .collect(),
300	                locked_packages: installed,
301	                virtual_packages: self.virtual_packages,
302	                channel_priority: self.channel_priority,
303	                exclude_newer,
304	                strategy: self.strategy,
305	                constraints: constrains_match_specs,
306	                ..rattler_solve::SolverTask::from_iter(solvable_records)
307	            };
308	
309	            let solver_result = rattler_solve::resolvo::Solver.solve(task)?;
310	
311	            // Convert the results back into pixi records.
312	            Ok::<_, SolveCondaEnvironmentError>(
313	                solver_result
314	                    .records
315	                    .into_iter()
316	                    .filter_map(|record| {
317	                        if let Some(source_record) = url_to_source_package.remove(&record.url) {
318	                            // This is a source package, we want to return the source record
319	                            // instead of the binary record.
320	                            return Some(PixiRecord::Source(source_record.0.clone()));
321	                        } else if let Some(_dev_source) = url_to_dev_source.remove(&record.url) {
322	                            // This is a dev source, we don't want to return it.
323	                            return None;
324	                        }
325	
326	                        Some(PixiRecord::Binary(record))
327	                    })
328	                    .collect_vec(),
329	            )
330	        })
331	        .await;
332	
333	        // Error out if the background task failed or was canceled.
334	        match solve_result.map_err(JoinError::try_into_panic) {
335	            Err(Err(_)) => Err(CommandDispatcherError::Cancelled),
336	            Err(Ok(panic)) => std::panic::resume_unwind(panic),
337	            Ok(Err(err)) => Err(CommandDispatcherError::Failed(err)),
338	            Ok(Ok(result)) => Ok(result),
339	        }
340	    }
341	}
342	
343	/// Generates a unique URL for a source record.
344	fn unique_url(source: &SourceRecord) -> Url {
345	    let mut url = source.manifest_source.identifiable_url();
346	
347	    // Add unique identifiers to the URL.
348	    url.query_pairs_mut()
349	        .append_pair("name", source.package_record.name.as_source())
350	        .append_pair("version", &source.package_record.version.as_str())
351	        .append_pair("build", &source.package_record.build)
352	        .append_pair("subdir", &source.package_record.subdir);
353	
354	    url
355	}
356	
357	/// Generates a unique URL for a dev source record.
358	fn unique_dev_source_url(dev_source: &pixi_record::DevSourceRecord) -> Url {
359	    let mut url = dev_source.source.identifiable_url();
360	
361	    // Add unique identifiers to the URL.
362	    let mut pairs = url.query_pairs_mut();
363	    pairs.append_pair("name", dev_source.name.as_source());
364	
365	    for (key, value) in &dev_source.variants {
366	        pairs.append_pair(&format!("_{key}"), value.to_string().as_str());
367	    }
368	
369	    drop(pairs);
370	
371	    url
372	}
373	
374	/// Generates a unique build string for a dev source record based on its variants.
375	/// Uses a hash of the variants to ensure uniqueness when multiple variants exist.
376	fn dev_source_build_string(dev_source: &pixi_record::DevSourceRecord) -> String {
377	    use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;
378	    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
379	
380	    // Hash the variants to create a stable, unique build string
381	    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();
382	    dev_source.variants.hash(&mut hasher);
383	    let hash = hasher.finish();
384	    format!("{hash:x}")
385	}

40	/// desired, use [`SolveCondaEnvironmentSpec`] instead.
41	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
42	#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
43	pub struct PixiEnvironmentSpec {
44	    pub name: Option<String>,

127	    #[instrument(

130	            name = self.name.as_deref().unwrap_or("unspecified"),
131	            platform = %self.build_environment.host_platform,

134	    pub async fn solve(
135	        self,
136	        command_queue: CommandDispatcher,
137	        gateway_reporter: Option<Box<dyn rattler_repodata_gateway::Reporter>>,
138	    ) -> Result<Vec<PixiRecord>, CommandDispatcherError<SolvePixiEnvironmentError>> {
139	        // Process dev sources to get their metadata (before dependencies are moved)
140	        let dev_source_records = self.process_dev_sources(&command_queue).await?;
141	
142	        let exclude_newer = self.exclude_newer.clone();

144	        // Split the requirements into source and binary requirements.
145	        let (dev_source_source_specs, dev_source_binary_specs) =
146	            DevSourceRecord::split_into_source_and_binary_requirements(

content.text: I found the core construction in `pixi_manifest::FeaturesExt::exclude_newer_config_resolved`: it sets the workspace cutoff as the default, then adds channel cutoffs keyed by `channel.channel.to_string()`. Existing tests only verify direct lookup with those raw names, not whether the keys match the canonical channel strings used on real repodata records. Next I’m checking the channel representation/matching details so the regression test targets the actual mismatch.

810	/// Parses a v3 whl record from its raw json representation.
811	/// The key is an extension-less `ArchiveIdentifier`.
812	fn parse_v3_whl_record_raw<'i>(
813	    filename: PackageFilename<'i>,
814	    raw_json: &'i RawValue,
815	    base_url: Option<&str>,
816	    channel: &Channel,
817	    channel_name: Option<String>,
818	    subdir: &str,
819	) -> io::Result<RepoDataRecord> {
820	    let WhlPackageRecord {
821	        url,
822	        mut package_record,
823	    } = serde_json::from_str(raw_json.get())?;
824	    if package_record.subdir.is_empty() {
825	        package_record.subdir = subdir.to_owned();
826	    }
827	    let archive_id: ArchiveIdentifier = filename.filename.parse().map_err(|e| {
828	        io::Error::new(
829	            io::ErrorKind::InvalidData,
830	            format!("invalid archive identifier '{}': {}", filename.filename, e),
831	        )
832	    })?;
833	    let identifier = DistArchiveIdentifier::new(archive_id, WheelArchiveType::Whl);
834	    let url = match url {
835	        UrlOrPath::Path(path) => compute_package_url(
836	            &channel
837	                .base_url
838	                .url()
839	                .join(&format!("{subdir}/"))
840	                .expect("failed determine repo_base_url"),
841	            base_url,
842	            &path,
843	        ),
844	        UrlOrPath::Url(url) => url,
845	    };
846	
847	    Ok(RepoDataRecord {
848	        url,
849	        channel: channel_name,
850	        package_record,
851	        identifier,
852	    })
853	}

Implemented the exclude-newer channel precedence fix without committing changes.

What changed:
- `FeaturesExt::exclude_newer_config(_resolved)` now receives a `ChannelConfig`.
- Channel-specific `exclude-newer` overrides are registered under both:
  - the manifest channel spelling, e.g. `conda-forge`
  - the resolved channel URL used by repodata records, e.g. `https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/`
- Updated all call sites to pass the appropriate workspace/group/environment channel config.
- Updated existing tests to use the new method signature.
- Removed the temporary regression test as requested.

Validation run:
- `cargo test -p pixi_core workspace::environment::tests::test_channel_specific_exclude_newer_uses_resolved_channel_url --no-default-features` passed before temporary test removal.

Note:
- `cargo fmt --package pixi_manifest --package pixi_core` could not run because `rustfmt` is not installed for the current toolchain.
